buyers.description: Mid-stage technology acquisition firm specializing in AI infrastructure. Acquires 3-5 companies per year. Previous acquisitions include ML pipeline tools and data platform startups.
buyers.notes: Prefers products with existing test suites and documentation. ROI-driven decision making.

buyers.description: Seed-stage AI startup building enterprise automation tools. Currently raising Series A. Needs a production-ready AI backend to accelerate product launch.
buyers.notes: Technical founder — will evaluate code quality and test coverage first. Impressed by 382+ tests at 100%.

buyers.description: Fortune 500 company with 15,000+ employees. Digital transformation initiative to deploy internal AI platform across 20 departments. Requires SOC2/GDPR compliance.
buyers.notes: Enterprise procurement cycle (60-90 days). Requires compliance documentation, security review, and architecture walkthrough. Budget already allocated for FY2026 Q3.

buyers.description: B2B SaaS platform with 500+ enterprise customers. Wants to embed AI orchestration into their existing product. 50-person engineering team.
buyers.notes: Interested in white-labeling the agent runtime. Needs custom agent development for their domain.

buyers.description: University-affiliated AI research lab. Working on multi-agent coordination and knowledge graph research. Needs the function library and memory stack for experiments.
buyers.notes: Academic budget constraints. EDUCATIONAL discount code applicable (40% off). Potential for joint research publication.

buyers.description: Cybersecurity consulting firm expanding into AI security. Wants to use Nexus Core's security module as foundation for their AI security assessment tool.
buyers.notes: Interested in penetration testing methodology. May want joint security audit of Nexus Core first.

buyers.description: Startup studio that builds 2-3 AI companies per year. Wants to use Nexus Core as the technical foundation for new spinouts. Budget-conscious but recurring buyer potential.
buyers.notes: Evaluating for Q3 2026 spinout. May license per-spinout or buy Enterprise License for unlimited use. LAUNCH2026 discount applicable.

buyers.description: Open-source foundation interested in the kernel and memory components. May sponsor development or purchase select modules.
buyers.notes: Not primary target — low budget but good for community credibility and referrals.

buyers.description: Technology value-added reseller serving enterprise clients across Southeast Asia. Wants to resell Nexus Core as part of their AI transformation package.
buyers.notes: Interested in white-label/reseller arrangement. Has 20+ enterprise clients waiting for AI solutions. LINE integration is a key selling point for Thai market.

buyers.description: Robotics startup building autonomous warehouse systems. Needs a lightweight, deterministic kernel for edge deployment. Interested in the compiled kernel modules.
buyers.notes: Needs Python 3.11+ compatibility. Edge deployment constraints (Raspberry Pi / Jetson). May commission custom function development for robotics-specific operations.

Platinum.description: High-value buyers ($50k+). VIP handling, direct CTO-to-CTO engagement, custom demo

Gold.description: Medium-value buyers ($15k-$50k). Standard engagement with technical deep-dive

Silver.description: Lower-value buyers ($5k-$15k). Self-service with email support

notes: All buyers pre-qualified by budget range and interest alignment
Platinum buyers get direct engineering team access
Engagement history updated after each interaction
Priority re-evaluated weekly based on engagement signals
Referral program available — existing buyers get 15% referral fee
